description: Add 11 new anonymous telemetry events to the MCP server covering plugin lifecycle (install, remove, update, review), permission changes, search, settings, install failures, and server updates. All events follow the existing privacy contract: no PII, no plugin names, no tool names, no file paths, no user content — only session IDs, coarse enums, bucketed counts, and booleans. Events use the existing trackEvent() infrastructure with the same opt-out mechanisms.

userStories.title: Add plugin_installed and plugin_install_failed telemetry events
userStories.description: Emit a plugin_installed event after successful plugin installation (both side panel and CLI paths) and a plugin_install_failed event on failure, with source discrimination and error categorization.
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('plugin_installed', ...) is called after successful installPlugin in handlePluginInstall with properties session_id and source: 'side_panel'
trackEvent('plugin_install_failed', ...) is called in the catch block of handlePluginInstall with properties session_id, source: 'side_panel', and error_category
error_category is one of 'npm_failure', 'invalid_plugin', or 'timeout' based on the error message pattern
No plugin names, package names, or error messages are included in any event properties
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add trackEvent calls in handlePluginInstall)
- platform/mcp-server/src/telemetry.ts (import getSessionId — already exported)

Context:
The existing telemetry system lives in `platform/mcp-server/src/telemetry.ts`. It exports `trackEvent(event, properties?)` and `getSessionId()`. Events are fire-and-forget, no PII. The PostHog client is initialized with `disableGeoip: true`.

The side panel install flow goes through `handlePluginInstall` in extension-handlers.ts (line 916). It calls `installPlugin(params.name, state, callbacks.onReload)` and on success sends a `plugins.changed` notification.

Changes:
In `handlePluginInstall`, after the successful `installPlugin` call (around line 928), before sending the result back, add:
```
trackEvent('plugin_installed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  source: 'side_panel',
});
```

In the catch block (around line 946), add:
```
const errorMsg = err instanceof Error ? err.message.toLowerCase() : '';
const error_category = errorMsg.includes('timed out') ? 'timeout' : errorMsg.includes('not a valid opentabs plugin') ? 'invalid_plugin' : 'npm_failure';
trackEvent('plugin_install_failed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  source: 'side_panel',
  error_category,
});
```

Add imports at the top of extension-handlers.ts:
```
import { getSessionId, trackEvent } from './telemetry.js';
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT include any plugin name, package name, or error message text in event properties
- Do NOT modify the existing error handling flow — the trackEvent calls are additive only
- The `source` field is always 'side_panel' here because the CLI install path (`opentabs plugin install`) does NOT go through this handler — it calls `npm install -g` directly. CLI telemetry is out of scope for this story.
- Do NOT add trackEvent to handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier — that gets its own event in a later story

userStories.title: Add plugin_removed telemetry event
userStories.description: Emit a plugin_removed event after successful plugin removal from both the side panel remove handler and the remove-by-specifier handler (for failed plugins).
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('plugin_removed', ...) is called after successful removePlugin in handlePluginRemove with properties session_id, source: 'side_panel', was_failed: false, plugin_source matching the removed plugin's source

trackEvent('plugin_removed', ...) is called after successful removeFailedPlugin in handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier with properties session_id, source: 'side_panel', was_failed: true, plugin_source: 'unknown'
plugin_source is 'npm' or 'local' for successful plugins, 'unknown' for failed plugins
No plugin names or specifier paths are included in event properties
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add trackEvent calls in handlePluginRemove and handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier)

Context:
`handlePluginRemove` (line 986) calls `removePlugin(pluginName, state, callbacks.onReload)`. Before calling removePlugin, we can look up the plugin to determine its source. After success, emit the event.

`handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier` (line 1026) calls `removeFailedPlugin(params.specifier, state, callbacks.onReload)`. Failed plugins don't have a registered source, so use 'unknown'.

Changes:
In `handlePluginRemove`, before the `removePlugin` call, look up the plugin source:
```
const existingPlugin = state.registry.plugins.get(pluginName);
const pluginSource = existingPlugin?.source ?? 'unknown';
```

After successful `removePlugin` (after line 999, before the `callbacks.queryExtension` call), add:
```
trackEvent('plugin_removed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  source: 'side_panel',
  was_failed: false,
  plugin_source: pluginSource === 'npm' ? 'npm' : pluginSource === 'local' ? 'local' : 'unknown',
});
```

In `handlePluginRemoveBySpecifier`, after the successful `removeFailedPlugin` call (after line 1038, before the plugins.changed send), add:
```
trackEvent('plugin_removed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  source: 'side_panel',
  was_failed: true,
  plugin_source: 'unknown',
});
```

Constraints:
- `trackEvent` and `getSessionId` imports should already be present from US-001
- Do NOT include plugin names or specifier paths in the event
- The lookup of `existingPlugin` must happen BEFORE `removePlugin` is called, because after removal the plugin is no longer in the registry
- plugin_source must be one of 'npm', 'local', or 'unknown' — never the raw `source` field value which might change in the future

userStories.title: Add plugin_updated and plugin_reviewed telemetry events
userStories.description: Emit plugin_updated after successful plugin update from the side panel, and plugin_reviewed after successful plugin_mark_reviewed MCP tool call.
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('plugin_updated', ...) is called after successful updatePlugin in handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry with properties session_id and source: 'side_panel'
trackEvent('plugin_reviewed', ...) is called after successful plugin review in handlePluginMarkReviewed with properties session_id, permission_set ('ask' or 'auto'), and review_source: 'agent'
No plugin names or versions are included in event properties
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add trackEvent in handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts (add trackEvent in handlePluginMarkReviewed)

Context:
`handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry` is at line 951 in extension-handlers.ts. After the successful `updatePlugin` call (line 963), emit the event.

`handlePluginMarkReviewed` is at line 726 in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts. After consuming the review token and persisting the permission (around line 810), emit the event. The `permission` variable (line 734) holds 'ask' or 'auto'. The review always comes from an AI agent (the MCP tool is called by agents, not the side panel), so review_source is always 'agent'.

Changes:
In `handlePluginUpdateFromRegistry`, after the successful `updatePlugin` call:
```
trackEvent('plugin_updated', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  source: 'side_panel',
});
```

In `handlePluginMarkReviewed` in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts, after `void savePluginPermissions(...)` (line 810), add:
```
trackEvent('plugin_reviewed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  permission_set: permission as string,
  review_source: 'agent',
});
```

Add imports to mcp-tool-dispatch.ts:
```
import { getSessionId, trackEvent } from './telemetry.js';
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT include plugin names, versions, or review token data in event properties
- The review_source is always 'agent' because handlePluginMarkReviewed is a platform MCP tool only callable by AI agents. The side panel has a separate permission-setting flow (handleConfigSetPluginPermission) which is NOT a review — it's a permission change covered by US-004.
- Do NOT confuse the side panel's setPluginPermission with reviewedVersion (which also sets permission) — these are different flows. The side panel flow triggers permission_changed (US-004), not plugin_reviewed.

userStories.title: Add permission_changed and skip_permissions_changed telemetry events
userStories.description: Emit permission_changed when a plugin-level permission is changed via the side panel, and skip_permissions_changed when the skip-permissions flag is toggled.
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('permission_changed', ...) is called in handleConfigSetPluginPermission with properties session_id, target ('plugin' or 'browser'), and new_permission
trackEvent('skip_permissions_changed', ...) is called in handleConfigSetSkipPermissions with properties session_id and enabled
target is 'browser' when pluginName === 'browser', otherwise 'plugin'
No plugin names are included in event properties
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add trackEvent calls in handleConfigSetPluginPermission and handleConfigSetSkipPermissions)

Context:
`handleConfigSetPluginPermission` is at line 507. After the permission is updated in state and persisted (after `callbacks.onPluginPermissionsPersist()` at line 552), emit the event. The `pluginName` variable (line 519) is either 'browser' or a plugin name. The `permission` variable (line 520) is the new permission ('off', 'ask', or 'auto').

`handleConfigSetSkipPermissions` is at line 572. After `state.skipPermissions = skipPermissions` (line 589), emit the event.

Changes:
In `handleConfigSetPluginPermission`, after `callbacks.onPluginPermissionsPersist()` (line 552):
```
trackEvent('permission_changed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  target: pluginName === 'browser' ? 'browser' : 'plugin',
  new_permission: permission,
});
```

In `handleConfigSetSkipPermissions`, after `state.skipPermissions = skipPermissions` (line 589):
```
trackEvent('skip_permissions_changed', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  enabled: skipPermissions,
});
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT include the plugin name in permission_changed — only 'plugin' or 'browser' as the target
- Do NOT include an old_permission field — per privacy audit, transition pairs create a behavioral fingerprint
- The trackEvent/getSessionId imports should already be in extension-handlers.ts from US-001
- This handler is also called when handlePluginMarkReviewed indirectly triggers a permission persist — but that's a different code path (mcp-tool-dispatch.ts), so there's no double-counting

userStories.title: Add plugin_search telemetry event
userStories.description: Emit plugin_search after search completion in the side panel search handler, with a bucketed result count.
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('plugin_search', ...) is called after successful searchNpmPlugins in handlePluginSearch with properties session_id, source: 'side_panel', and result_count_bucket
result_count_bucket is '0' when results.length === 0, '1-5' when 1-5, '6+' when 6 or more
No search query text is included in event properties
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add trackEvent in handlePluginSearch)

Context:
`handlePluginSearch` is at line 893. After the successful `searchNpmPlugins(query ?? undefined)` call (line 905), before sending the result, emit the event.

Changes:
After `const results = await searchNpmPlugins(query ?? undefined)` (line 905):
```
const resultCount = results.length;
const result_count_bucket = resultCount === 0 ? '0' : resultCount <= 5 ? '1-5' : '6+';
trackEvent('plugin_search', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
  source: 'side_panel',
  result_count_bucket,
});
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT include the query text, had_query boolean, or any search parameters — per privacy audit, these leak search intent
- Do NOT include the exact result count — only the bucket
- Do NOT emit on search failure (the catch block) — only on successful search

userStories.title: Add plugin_configured telemetry event
userStories.description: Emit plugin_configured after successful plugin settings save from both the side panel JSON-RPC handler and the HTTP endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('plugin_configured', ...) is called after successful settings save in handleConfigSetPluginSettings with properties session_id, source: 'side_panel', and had_required_fields
trackEvent('plugin_configured', ...) is called after successful settings save in the POST /plugin-settings HTTP handler with properties session_id, source: 'http', and had_required_fields
had_required_fields is a boolean indicating whether the plugin's configSchema has any required fields
No plugin names, field names, field counts, or field values are included in event properties

userStories.title: Add server_update_available and server_update_applied telemetry events
userStories.description: Emit server_update_available when the version check detects a newer CLI version, and server_update_applied after a successful server self-update.
acceptanceCriteria: trackEvent('server_update_available', ...) is called in checkServerUpdate when a newer version is detected, with only session_id
trackEvent('server_update_applied', ...) is called in handleServerSelfUpdate after a successful npm install, with only session_id
No version numbers are included in event properties
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.ts (add trackEvent in checkServerUpdate)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (add trackEvent in handleServerSelfUpdate)

Context:
`checkServerUpdate` is at line 124 in version-check.ts. When `isNewer(version, latest)` is true (line 133), a new version is available. Emit the event there.

`handleServerSelfUpdate` is at line 1074 in extension-handlers.ts. After the successful `spawnSync` npm install (line 1091), when `result.status === 0`, emit the event before sending the success response.

Changes:
In `checkServerUpdate` in version-check.ts, after `state.serverUpdate = { latestVersion: latest, ... }` (line 134):
```
trackEvent('server_update_available', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
});
```

Add imports to version-check.ts:
```
import { getSessionId, trackEvent } from './telemetry.js';
```

In `handleServerSelfUpdate` in extension-handlers.ts, after verifying success (after the error check at line 1095), before sending the success response (before line 1104):
```
trackEvent('server_update_applied', {
  session_id: getSessionId(),
});
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT include version numbers (current or latest) in event properties
- Do NOT include update commands or package names
- checkServerUpdate runs once at startup in the background — the event fires at most once per server session
- The trackEvent/getSessionId imports should already be in extension-handlers.ts from US-001

userStories.title: Add unit tests for all new telemetry events
userStories.description: Add tests verifying that all 11 new telemetry events are emitted with correct properties, and that no privacy-violating properties (plugin names, search queries, error messages, version numbers) are included.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify trackEvent is called with correct event name and properties for each of the 11 events: plugin_installed, plugin_install_failed, plugin_removed, plugin_updated, plugin_reviewed, permission_changed, skip_permissions_changed, plugin_search, plugin_configured, server_update_available, server_update_applied
Tests verify no event includes plugin names, search query text, error message text, or version numbers in properties
Tests verify the error_category classification in plugin_install_failed covers 'npm_failure', 'invalid_plugin', and 'timeout'
Tests verify result_count_bucket bucketing: 0 results → '0', 3 results → '1-5', 10 results → '6+'
